g.text: Global Queue Bottleneck Analysis: Thread Serialization vs. Work Stealing

div.p: Centralized resources create a bottleneck that scales O(N) with thread count. Distributed local resources with Work Stealing provide O(1) scalability. In the Coroutine Engine, this is the difference between linear performance scaling and thread starvation.

g.text: Cache Line Bounces | High (L3 Shared) | Low (L1 Local)

text.tspan: // Thread 1 pops -> Invalidates Cache Line on Threads 2-8
// Result: Stall on every memory access.
// Thread 1 reads LQ1 -> L1 Cache Hit (Fast)
// Thread 2 steals from LQ1 -> Atomic CAS (Rare)
// Result: Execution at hardware memory speed.
// Thread 1 pops -> Invalidates Cache Line on Threads 2-8
// Result: Stall on every memory access.
// Thread 1 reads LQ1 -> L1 Cache Hit (Fast)
// Thread 2 steals from LQ1 -> Atomic CAS (Rare)
// Result: Execution at hardware memory speed.

div.p: T1 Accessing T2: Blocked T3: Blocked T4: Blocked
T2 Accessing T1: Context Switch T3: Blocked T4: Blocked
